reasoning: The Chicago Fed's NFCI Credit Subindex is constructed using a dynamic factor model that aggregates numerous financial time series. By its nature, it is heavily smoothed and exhibits strong autocorrelation (momentum). Over the last 4 weeks leading up to the 2026-02-27 cutoff, the index has experienced persistent, accelerating increases: from a trough of -0.036 on Jan 30 to -0.011 on Feb 27. The first derivative reached an extremely high +0.009 in the latest week. For the March 8 resolution (a 1-week horizon), this momentum makes it highly probable the index will continue to climb and exceed -0.011. For the March 31 resolution (a 4-week horizon), uncertainty increases. However, examining the historical data from the past year shows that peaks and troughs in this index typically occur in cycles lasting 2 to 4 months (e.g., Aug-2025 to Dec-2025). Since the current upswing from the January trough has only lasted one month so far, the index will likely still be in its ascending phase or near its apex by late March. Thus, the March 27 reading corresponding to the March 31 date is also very likely to remain above the -0.011 threshold, although there is a larger margin of uncertainty in case a local cyclical reversal occurs earlier than expected.
